role: Former Minister of Foreign Affairs (2021–2024); died in helicopter crash
bio: Hossein Amir-Abdollahian was Iran's Foreign Minister under [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) from August 2021 until his death in May 2024. He died alongside Raisi in the helicopter crash on 19 May 2024 in East Azerbaijan. A career diplomat who specialised in Arab and African affairs, he served as Deputy Foreign Minister for Arab and African Affairs under [Manouchehr Mottaki](/people/manouchehr-mottaki) (2011–2016) and was known for his close ties to Hezbollah, Hamas, and the [IRGC Quds Force](/orgs/irgc-quds-force). He was removed from his deputy role by [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif) in 2016, then became a senior adviser to [Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf](/people/mohammad-bagher-ghalibaf) at the Majlis. His foreign policy prioritised the 'resistance axis' and East-facing engagement with Russia and China.
editorial_notes: Born 1964. Died 19 May 2024 in helicopter crash alongside Raisi and others. Removed from deputy FM role by Zarif in 2016. Sanctioned by US and EU.
